title: Payment cache degradation under load — stale pricing observed
description: During the Nov 1 Black Friday load test, ~8% of checkout requests showed stale prices. Root cause: in-memory cache per pod, invalidation not propagating across replicas after a price update event.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: Confirmed root cause — each pod has its own cache instance, invalidation only hits the pod that received the update event.

comments.author: marcus.lee
comments.body: Given Black Friday is in 3 weeks, let's move to a shared cache rather than patch invalidation logic. Redis is already in our stack for sessions.

title: Choose datastore for new orders table
description: New orders feature needs a persistent store. Need to decide between Postgres (relational, ACID) and MongoDB (flexible schema, already used by the catalog service).

comments.author: wei.zhang
comments.body: Wrote up RFC-004 comparing both options given the transactional requirements around order + payment + line items.

title: Modernize auth for mobile app launch
description: Umbrella ticket for auth modernization ahead of mobile launch: JWT migration, refresh tokens, and rate limiting after the credential-stuffing incident.

comments.author: amara.okafor
comments.body: Phase 3 (rate limiting) shipped in PR #190 following the Aug 26 credential-stuffing attempt. Opened SEC-40 to revisit the specific limit once we have usage data.

title: Revisit login rate-limit thresholds with real usage data
description: SEC-33 shipped a 5-attempts/15-min login rate limit based on OWASP's general default, not validated against our own traffic. Revisit once we have a few weeks of data — concern raised in PR #190 review about shared-office-IP false positives.

title: Split monolith into services per RFC-007
description: Umbrella ticket tracking the phased monolith extraction described in RFC-007. Phase 1: billing. Phase 2: notifications. Future phases TBD based on how the first two go.

comments.author: tom.harrison
comments.body: Phase 1 (billing) done in PR #310 — picked billing first since it has the cleanest bounded context of any module.

title: Oct 2 incident: notifications latency cascaded into billing failures
description: Post-incident follow-up. billing-service's synchronous, timeout-less call to notifications-service caused billing request pile-up when notifications got slow. Needs a circuit breaker.

comments.author: tom.harrison
comments.body: Circuit breaker added in PR #315, fails open (skips notification rather than blocking billing) and alerts on trip.

title: Support stacked promo codes for Dec campaign
description: Marketing wants to combine a seasonal discount with referral credits for the December campaign. Requires calculate_discount() to accept multiple codes.
